stdout:
I’m checking the current diff and the repo quality gates together, then I’ll compare the result against the issue criteria and prior failed reviews.

## Issue: No-op gated retries still bump `updated_at` via gate/assignee flags
**File:** `/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/crates/jit/src/main.rs:595-624`, `/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/crates/jit/src/commands/gate.rs:118-146`, `/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/crates/jit/src/commands/gate.rs:183-198`, `/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/crates/jit/src/commands/issue.rs:463-466`, `/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/crates/jit/src/commands/issue.rs:547-550`, `/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/crates/jit/src/storage/json.rs:409-412`
**Severity:** Medium
**Problem:** The fix correctly makes `update_issue()` itself idempotent for title/description/priority/label no-ops, but `jit issue update` still performs `--add-gate`, `--remove-gate`, `--assignee`, and `--unassign` through separate helper calls before `update_issue()`. Those helpers always call `save_issue()`, even when nothing actually changed (`already_exist`, `not_found`, assigning the same assignee, unassigning an already-unassigned issue). On an already-gated `--state done` retry, those semantic no-ops will still bump `updated_at`, so the acceptance criterion is not fully met.
**Evidence:** `main.rs` invokes gate/assignee mutations before `executor.update_issue(...)`. `add_gates()` and `remove_gates()` track `already_exist`/`not_found` but still save unconditionally; `assign_issue()`/`unassign_issue()` also save unconditionally. `save_issue()` always rewrites `updated_at`. The new tests only cover the `update_issue()` field-edit path, not these other `issue update` flags.
**Suggested fix:** Make the gate/assignee helpers used by `issue update` short-circuit on semantic no-ops, or centralize no-op detection for the full `issue update` command before any save occurs.I found one more path worth checking from the current implementation: `issue update` can still hit separate gate/assignee helpers before the guarded state change path, so I’m verifying whether those helpers keep the retry truly no-op.

## Acceptance criteria

**Fail.** The original `gated -> gated` bug in the core `update_issue()` path is fixed, but the full `jit issue update ... --state done` command is still not idempotent across all update flags.

- **Met:** `crates/jit/src/commands/issue.rs:221-224`, `279-280`, and `714-723` now compute a real dirty flag for title/description/priority/label edits and only save/log when something actually changed or when there is a real transition into `Gated`. That addresses the prior failed review about idempotent metadata flags.
- **Met:** `crates/jit/src/commands/issue.rs:726-731` still returns `TransitionBlockedError::gates(...)`, so blocking-gate guidance is still surfaced on retry.
- **Met:** `crates/jit/src/commands/issue.rs:950-1101` adds regression tests for pure retries, real field edits on gated retries, and idempotent same-value field edits.
- **Met:** `crates/jit/src/commands/issue.rs:719-723` still emits an `issue_state_changed` event for a genuine transition into `Gated`.
- **Not met:** `crates/jit/src/main.rs:587-624` runs `add_gates`, `remove_gates`, `assign_issue`, and `unassign_issue` before `update_issue()`. Those helpers still unconditionally save even on semantic no-ops: `crates/jit/src/commands/gate.rs:118-146`, `183-198`, `crates/jit/src/commands/issue.rs:463-466`, and `547-550`. Because `crates/jit/src/storage/json.rs:409-412` always rewrites `updated_at`, commands like `jit issue update <id> --state done --add-gate <already-present>` or `--unassign` on an already-unassigned gated issue still advance `updated_at` despite no real change. That leaves the “no-op retry must not bump `updated_at`” requirement incomplete.

## Architecture and separation of concerns

No new layer violations. The change stays in command/storage orchestration: `main.rs` coordinates the command, `commands/*.rs` call the storage layer, and no I/O is pushed into domain code.

## Functional paradigm

Acceptable overall. The new dirty-check in `crates/jit/src/commands/issue.rs:187-224` is a clean, explicit way to distinguish semantic edits from idempotent flags.

## Correctness and invariants

**Blocking issue remains.** The audit-log invariant is fixed for title/description/priority/label retries, but the timestamp invariant is still broken for gate/assignee no-op flags because those paths save unconditionally before the guarded `handle_gate_blocking()` logic runs.

## Safety

No unsafe code touched, and I found no PID-related changes.

## Testing

The repository checks completed successfully: `cargo test --workspace --quiet`, `cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets --quiet`, `cargo fmt --all -- --check`, and `./scripts/jit-validate.sh` all passed; `jit-validate` only emitted allowed label warnings.

Test coverage is still incomplete for the remaining bug: the new tests in `crates/jit/src/commands/issue.rs:950-1101` do not cover `issue update` retries that include idempotent `--add-gate`, `--remove-gate`, `--assignee`, or `--unassign` flags.

## Error handling

No new error-handling regressions in the reviewed change. The blocking path still returns a contextual gate error from `crates/jit/src/commands/issue.rs:726-731`.

## CLI contract

No `--json` regression found in the changed code.

## Documentation

No new public API was introduced, so I found no blocking documentation issue.

## Prior review feedback

The most recent failed review is addressed in the `update_issue()` field-edit path:

- The previous “mixed metadata edits are dropped” regression is fixed by persisting when `has_field_edits` is true (`crates/jit/src/commands/issue.rs:275-280`, `714-718`) and by `test_update_issue_field_edits_persist_when_done_blocked_on_gated` (`1045-1095`).
- The previous “provided flags vs actual changes” bug is fixed by comparing original and final field values (`187-224`) and by `test_update_issue_idempotent_field_on_gated_retry_is_noop` (`1097-1101` and following).

But a new unresolved no-op path remains through the separate gate/assignee helpers described above.

## JIT issue management

`issue.dependencies` is empty, so there are no unmet prerequisites blocking this work. `./scripts/jit-validate.sh` passed within the review run, with only allowed label warnings.

VERDICT: FAIL

Gate result: FAILED
